Primary_Work_Scope: Occupancy Only - No Building Alterations or Renovations
Work_Description:
I'm the operator of Cameo Hotel and Suites in the building at 1572 Barrington Street. The building contains commercial ground floor spaces, plus 21 apartment units above. I lease the residential portion of the building only. The residential units have been operated as hotel suites / short term rentals since the property opened for business in May 2023. This use is permitted by zoning, but we just learned that the landlord did not apply to change the permitted use from residential to hotel/short-term before he passed away unexpectedly in December 2023. 

As part of the new regulations for registration of short term rental properties, we have now been asked to provide a confirmation letter from the city confirming this is a permitted use of the building. We understood that what is needed is a new occupancy permit noting the current use. We would like to obtain this permit for the building as 1572 Barrington street, as well as the building at 1669 Granville Street (which we are also leasing fully under a similar arrangement with the same deceased landlord).

Work_Description:
We have just purchased this home which is zoned R1. There is a fully separate one-bedroom suite in the basement of the house that has been present before the zoning bylaws came into effect. I am applying for this occupancy permit to legalize the suite's use as a 'nonconforming secondary suite'. The house had been owned by the sellers' family since 1932 and passed down to the next generation which is why no one has ever applied for an occupancy permit prior to this. The sellers have provided me with dated photos showing the suite from 1961and 1962 as proof of its existence at this time, the baby in the photo is the seller himself. I have attached these photos in the supplemental documents.

**I have added a copy of an email from a 311 planner explaining that it is an occupancy permit that I need, not a renovation permit. This is the same information I had also received over the phone previously, I believe that request was #7734045.

I represent Outfront Media (the applicant), which is an operator of billboard signage within HRM.  Outfront Media currently operates an existing billboard sign at 560 Windmill Road, Dartmouth, NS,  which is static in nature (a traditional paper posted billboard).  It contains two display faces (each 10'x20' in dimensions), one facing North and one facing South, directed at traffic travelling along Victoria Rd.  We are seeking to convert the North display face to a digital method of display (a.k.a a variable message sign or VMS), which will measure the same dimensions of 10'x20'.   The south facing display face, will remain operational as a static display face (no changes).  We will maintain the existing sign foundation and upright vertical support beam as they are now. As such, the height of the sign structure will not change from the height of the existing sign structure present now.   We feel the proposed conversion of the North display face meets all the requirements of the Dartmouth Land Use By-Law and the regulations specific to VMS Application Guidelines.  Specifically, in terms of the required setback from the closest signalized intersection, at Windmill Rd and Victoria Rd,  because the VMS is setback more than 21m from the Victoria Rd  street line (a 60km/h roadway), the sign structure would not be considered in a prohibited area.  Further to this, the zoning of the property is I-2 (Industrial Zoning), which allows for VMS billboards.  The VMS will have a light sensor and the maximum light output shall be no more than 3.2lux (0.3 ft candles) above ambient light levels.  The display face shall not display animation, shall not flash or scroll, and message sequencing will not be utilized.  Message transition time will be instantaneous.

The existing porch has approximately a 2 foot cantilever over the beam before attaching to the stairs.

The hope is that the same configuration can be maintained so that the current size of the porch remain the same. There is a support column from the porch roof that is supported by the current footing and column.  The column would be upgraded from the current 4x4 to a 6x6, and the current 2x6 double laminate beam would be replaced with a triple 2x8 beam. 

The size of the deck platform in this porch is 65' x 98'.  Railing height will be 36'.  The deck framing will be constructed from 2x8' material.

This permit application is for the demolition of the existing structure at 193 Portlands St. 

This is an old dentistry which belonged to the gunman in the April 2020 attacks across nova scotia. We hope we can expedite the demolition of this building and get the structure taken down as we are all aware of the impact this place has on the community.

We will begin with preliminary abatement work of the interior asbestos and exterior shingles containing lead paint.

I have checked that work in the municipal right of way will be present due to the buildings proximity to the side walk. 

I have also checked that we will be capping water and sewer. We are hoping the city will work with us on this one due to the history of the building and the process we are hoping to follow. We are hoping to cap the services within the existing foundation for now to avoid disruption to the busy street/bus stop located in front of the building. There will be a future development on this lot which will require upgrades to the services which would allow us to be in the street once with lane drops as opposed to twice. I have attached a lot plan showing our access from Prince Albert Rd to avoid Portland St disruption. We will detail these items out in a CMP as required.

Feel free to contact me directly (902)-943-5105 or thomas@brunobuilders.ca

This building is currently a triplex. A demolition permit is required for conversion back to a single family home for the owners occupancy. The structure started as a single family home and then the previous owner added a two story two level garage to one side of the building in about 2008. This garage was then converted to two apartments in 2014. Since separation with me wife I need to get those two apartments to add to the third to create one single family home for myself and children for my own occupancy following separation from my wife. 

never finished and was part of the house I occupy with interior access on both levels from my side of the house. When we bought the building we put in a fire wall to separate the garage from the house and turned this garage into two one bed apartments one on top of the other with outside access. 

My elderly parents and older sister need to come live with me permanently. To accommodate them, I am applying for a demolition permit to tear out the two apartments and create one larger house out of all the apartments. We don't plan to change the foot print of the building or load bearing walls, but we need to demolish the two apartments which are 530 sq ft apartments stacked one on top of the other to extend the two living floors from my side of the structure.

Primary_Work_Scope: Occupancy Only - No Building Alterations or Renovations
Work_Description:
Occupancy Permit Application for:

We purchased the property in May 2021, which contains a small cabin (floor plan and images attached) that is a remnant structure of the original homestead. The majority of the original homestead was demolished by the previous owners, and they retained a small portion (4.5m x 3.7m), containing a woodstove and a washroom connected to an onsite septic tank. 
We are applying for an occupancy permit with the intent of connecting power through Nova Scotia Power to this civic address. It was recommended by HRM that we apply for an occupancy permit for a “camp” which would allow HRM to reclassify the property from derelict single unit dwelling to a recreational camp. This reclassification would allow HRM to provide a letter to Nova Scotia Power to grant approval for power connection. 

We have included the following information to assist with our application: 
•	Floor Plan
•	Location Survey (indicating location of remnant structure)
•	Correspondence with HRM Planner 
•	Images of remnant structure

Primary_Work_Scope: Occupancy Only - No Building Alterations or Renovations
Work_Description:
We have just purchased this home which is zoned R1. There is a fully separate one-bedroom suite in the basement of the house that has been present before the zoning bylaws came into effect. I am applying for this occupancy permit to legalize the suite's use as a 'nonconforming secondary suite'. The house had been owned by the sellers' family since 1932 and passed down to the next generation which is why no one has ever applied for an occupancy permit prior to this. The sellers have provided me with dated photos showing the suite from 1961and 1962 as proof of its existence at this time, the baby in the photo is the seller himself. I have attached these photos in the supplemental documents.

**I have added a copy of an email from a 311 planner explaining that it is an occupancy permit that I need, not a renovation permit. This is the same information I had also received over the phone previously, I believe that request was #7734045.

Work_Description:
I'm the operator of Cameo Hotel and Suites in the building at 1572 Barrington Street. The building contains commercial ground floor spaces, plus 21 apartment units above. I lease the residential portion of the building only. The residential units have been operated as hotel suites / short term rentals since the property opened for business in May 2023. This use is permitted by zoning, but we just learned that the landlord did not apply to change the permitted use from residential to hotel/short-term before he passed away unexpectedly in December 2023. 

As part of the new regulations for registration of short term rental properties, we have now been asked to provide a confirmation letter from the city confirming this is a permitted use of the building. We understood that what is needed is a new occupancy permit noting the current use. We would like to obtain this permit for the building as 1572 Barrington street, as well as the building at 1669 Granville Street (which we are also leasing fully under a similar arrangement with the same deceased landlord).
